docs.title: My Blog App
docs.text: A full-stack blog platform built with Next.js 16, Prisma, MariaDB, NextAuth, and TailwindCSS + DaisyUI.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: My Blog App is a content-focused web application that allows authors to publish articles and readers to discover them. It features a clean public-facing blog, a credential-based authentication system, and an admin dashboard for managing content and users.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text: Feature Description Public Blog Browse the latest posts, a featured article, and most-viewed content Post Pages Individual post pages with view-count tracking REST API Paginated, searchable posts API endpoint Authentication Secure email/password login via NextAuth.js Admin Dashboard Overview metrics (posts, users, top content) User Management Role-based access control (ADMIN, EDITOR, VIEWER) Database ORM Prisma with MariaDB/MySQL

docs.title: Technology Stack
docs.text:
• Framework: Next.js 16 (App Router)
  • UI: React 19, TailwindCSS v4, DaisyUI v5
  • Auth: NextAuth.js v4
  • ORM: Prisma v7 with @prisma/adapter-mariadb
  • Database: MariaDB / MySQL
  • Language: TypeScript

docs.title: Quick Links
docs.text:
• Getting Started
  • Configuration
  • API Reference
  • Architecture Overview
  • Database Schema
  • Admin Panel Guide
  • Deployment

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: Thank you for your interest in contributing to My Blog App!

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
1. Fork the repository on GitHub.
  2. Clone your fork: 
    git clone https://github.com/<your-username>/my-nextjs-app.git
cd my-nextjs-app
  3. Follow the Getting Started guide to set up your local environment.

docs.title: Branching Strategy
docs.text:
Branch Purpose main Stable, production-ready code feat/<name> New features fix/<name> Bug fixes docs/<name> Documentation updates chore/<name> Maintenance tasks

Always branch off main and open a pull request back to main.

docs.title: Commit Messages
docs.text:
Follow the Conventional Commits spec:

<type>(<scope>): <short description>

Examples:
feat(posts): add category filter to post list
fix(auth): handle null user in authorize callback
docs(readme): update deployment instructions

Common types: feat, fix, docs, chore, refactor, test, style

docs.title: Code Style
docs.text:
• TypeScript: All new code must be TypeScript. Avoid any where possible.
  • Linting: Run npm run lint before committing. CI will block PRs with lint errors.
  • Formatting: Use Prettier (add it to the project if not already configured).

docs.title: Database Changes
docs.text:
If your change requires a schema update:

  1. Edit prisma/schema.prisma.
  2. Create a migration: npx prisma migrate dev --name describe-your-change
  3. Commit both the updated schema.prisma and the new migration files.

docs.title: Pull Request Checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] Code builds without errors (npm run build)
  • [ ] Lint passes (npm run lint)
  • [ ] All new behaviour is covered by tests (if a test suite is added)
  • [ ] Migration files are committed alongside schema changes
  • [ ] Documentation is updated if the change affects user-facing behaviour

docs.title: Reporting Bugs
docs.text:
Open an issue on GitHub with:

  • Steps to reproduce
  • Expected behaviour
  • Actual behaviour
  • Environment (OS, Node.js version, browser)

docs.title: Suggesting Features
docs.text: Open a GitHub Discussion or an issue labelled enhancement.

docs.title: Database Schema
docs.text:
The database is managed by Prisma with a MariaDB / MySQL provider.

Schema file: prisma/schema.prisma

erDiagram

docs.title: User
docs.text:
Represents an authenticated user of the platform.

Column Type Notes id Int Auto-increment primary key name String Display name email String Unique, used for login passwordHash String bcrypt-hashed password role Role ADMIN, EDITOR, or VIEWER createdAt DateTime Record creation timestamp updatedAt DateTime Auto-updated on change

docs.title: Post
docs.text:
Represents a blog article.

Column Type Notes id Int Auto-increment primary key title String Article title slug String Unique URL identifier excerpt String? Optional short summary content String Full body (Markdown or HTML) status PostStatus DRAFT or PUBLISHED isFeatured Boolean Highlights the post on the home page viewCount Int Incremented on each page view publishedAt DateTime? Nullable publish date authorId Int FK → User.id coverImageId Int? FK → Media.id

docs.title: Media
docs.text:
Stores uploaded images and files.

Column Type Notes id Int Auto-increment primary key url String Public URL of the asset alt String? Accessibility alt text mimeType String e.g. image/png size Int? File size in bytes uploadedById Int FK → User.id

docs.title: Category
docs.text:
Taxonomy for grouping posts.

Column Type Notes id Int Auto-increment primary key name String Unique display name slug String Unique URL identifier

docs.title: Tag
docs.text:
Lightweight labels for posts.

Column Type Notes id Int Auto-increment primary key name String Unique display name slug String Unique URL identifier

docs.title: PostCategory / PostTag
docs.text:
Explicit many-to-many join tables.

Column Type Notes postId Int FK → Post.id categoryId / tagId Int FK → Category.id / Tag.id — Composite PK (postId, categoryId) / (postId, tagId)

docs.title: Role
docs.text: Value Description ADMIN Full access: manage users, posts, and settings EDITOR Can create and manage posts VIEWER Read-only access (default for new users)

docs.title: PostStatus
docs.text: Value Description DRAFT Not publicly visible PUBLISHED Live and visible on the blog

docs.title: Migrations
docs.text:
Migration files live in prisma/migrations/. Each migration has a timestamped folder and a migration.sql file.

To create a new migration during development:

npx prisma migrate dev --name describe-your-change

To apply migrations in production:

npx prisma migrate deploy

docs.title: Architecture Overview
docs.text: My Blog App follows the Next.js App Router architecture with a clear separation between the public-facing blog, the admin panel, and the API layer.

docs.title: Public Blog Request
docs.text:
1. User visits / or /posts/[slug]
  2. Next.js Server Component calls a service function (e.g., getHomePageData)
  3. The service queries the database via the Prisma client singleton (src/lib/db.ts)
  4. Data is returned directly to the Server Component — no client-side fetching required

docs.title: API Request
docs.text:
1. Client calls GET /api/posts?page=1&q=react
  2. The route handler at src/app/api/posts/route.ts parses query parameters
  3. Calls getPaginatedPosts from the service layer
  4. Returns a JSON response

docs.title: Authentication Flow
docs.text:
1. User submits email/password on /login
  2. NextAuth's CredentialsProvider calls the authorize callback in src/lib/auth.ts
  3. The callback fetches the user from the database and verifies the password with bcryptjs
  4. On success, a JWT is issued containing the user's role
  5. The role is surfaced on the session object via the jwt and session callbacks
  6. Protected pages/routes check session.user.role before rendering

docs.title: Key Directories
docs.text: Path Purpose src/app/ All Next.js pages and API routes src/app/admin/ Role-protected admin section src/app/api/ REST API endpoints src/components/ Reusable React components src/lib/ Auth config, DB client, service functions prisma/ Schema, migrations, and seed script public/ Static assets docs/ Project documentation (MkDocs)

docs.title: Data Flow Diagram
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Browser
    participant NextJS as Next.js Server
    participant Service as Service Layer
    participant DB as MariaDB

    Browser->>NextJS: GET /
    NextJS->>Service: getHomePageData()
    Service->>DB: SELECT featured post, most viewed
    DB-->>Service: rows
    Service-->>NextJS: { featuredPost, mostViewed }
    NextJS-->>Browser: Rendered HTML

my-nextjs-app/

docs.title: Naming Conventions
docs.text: Item Convention Example Pages page.tsx in route folder app/posts/page.tsx Layouts layout.tsx in route folder app/admin/layout.tsx API routes route.ts in route folder app/api/posts/route.ts Components PascalCase .tsx FeaturedPost.tsx Services camelCase .ts posts.ts Path alias @/ → src/ import { prisma } from "@/lib/db"

docs.title: Admin Panel
docs.text: The admin panel is available at /admin and is restricted to users with the ADMIN role.

docs.title: Accessing the Admin Panel
docs.text:
1. Navigate to /login.
  2. Enter your admin credentials.
  3. After a successful login, you are redirected to the home page. Go to /admin manually or follow a link in the navigation.

Default credentials (development only):

Field Value Email admin@someshwarsutar.com Password Admin@123

Change these credentials immediately in a production environment.

docs.title: Dashboard (/admin)
docs.text:
The dashboard provides a high-level overview of the platform:

Metric Description Total Posts Count of all posts regardless of status Published Posts Count of posts with status = PUBLISHED Users Total number of registered users Top Viewed Post Title and view count of the most-viewed published post

docs.title: User Management (/admin/users)
docs.text:
Provides a searchable, paginated list of all users. Supports filtering by page (page) and keyword (q).

Planned features (not yet implemented):

  • Create / edit / delete users
  • Change user roles
  • Reset passwords

docs.title: Access Control
docs.text: The admin layout (src/app/admin/layout.tsx) enforces authentication and role checks before rendering any admin page. Unauthenticated users or users without the ADMIN role are redirected to /login.

docs.title: Navigation
docs.text: The admin section uses its own layout (src/app/admin/layout.tsx) which wraps all /admin pages and can include a sidebar, breadcrumbs, or top navigation specific to the admin area.

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text: My Blog App uses NextAuth.js v4 with a Credentials provider (email + password).

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. The user submits their email and password on /login.
  2. NextAuth calls the authorize callback defined in src/lib/auth.ts.
  3. The callback fetches the user from the database and uses bcryptjs.compare to verify the password against the stored hash.
  4. On success, NextAuth issues a JWT containing the user's id, name, email, and role.
  5. Subsequent requests include the JWT in a next-auth.session-token cookie.
  6. The role is attached to the session object so that pages and API routes can perform authorization checks.

docs.title: Session Shape
docs.text:
After sign-in, the session available via getServerSession or useSession looks like:

{
  user: {
    name: "Super Admin",
    email: "admin@someshwarsutar.com",
    role: "ADMIN"   // "ADMIN" | "EDITOR" | "VIEWER"
  },
  expires: "2026-07-01T00:00:00.000Z"
}

docs.title: Protecting Pages
docs.text:
Use getServerSession inside a Server Component or layout to guard protected routes:

import { getServerSession } from "next-auth";
import { authOptions } from "@/lib/auth";
import { redirect } from "next/navigation";

export default async function AdminLayout({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  const session = await getServerSession(authOptions);

  if (!session || session.user.role !== "ADMIN") {
    redirect("/login");
  }

  return <>{children}</>;
}

docs.title: Protecting API Routes
docs.text:
import { getServerSession } from "next-auth";
import { authOptions } from "@/lib/auth";
import { NextResponse } from "next/server";

export async function DELETE(req: Request) {
  const session = await getServerSession(authOptions);

  if (!session || session.user.role !== "ADMIN") {
    return NextResponse.json({ error: "Unauthorized" }, { status: 401 });
  }

  // ... handle request
}

docs.title: Password Hashing
docs.text:
Passwords are hashed with bcryptjs using a cost factor of 10. The seed script (prisma/seed.ts) demonstrates correct usage:

import bcrypt from "bcryptjs";
const passwordHash = await bcrypt.hash("plainTextPassword", 10);

Never store plain-text passwords. Always hash before saving to the database.

docs.title: Roles
docs.text: Role Capabilities ADMIN Full platform access EDITOR Manage and publish posts VIEWER Read-only (default)

docs.title: Configuration Reference
docs.text:
Auth options are exported from src/lib/auth.ts as authOptions and consumed by the NextAuth handler at src/app/api/auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts.

Key settings:

Option Value Provider CredentialsProvider Sign-in page /login JWT secret NEXTAUTH_SECRET env var Session strategy jwt (default)

docs.title: Deployment
docs.text: This guide covers deploying My Blog App to a production environment.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• A Linux server (Ubuntu 22.04+ recommended) or a PaaS platform
  • Node.js 20.x
  • MariaDB 10.6+ or MySQL 8.0+
  • A domain name (optional but recommended)

docs.title: Option 1: Vercel (Recommended for simplicity)
docs.text: Vercel is the easiest deployment target for Next.js applications.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1. Push your repository to GitHub.
  2. Go to vercel.com and import the repository.
  3. Set the following Environment Variables in the Vercel project settings:
Variable Value DATABASE_URL Your production MariaDB/MySQL connection string NEXTAUTH_SECRET A strong random secret (openssl rand -base64 32) NEXTAUTH_URL Your production domain, e.g. https://myblog.example.com
  1. Click Deploy.

Note: Vercel does not host databases. Use a managed database service such as PlanetScale, Railway, or Aiven.

docs.title: 1. Install Node.js
docs.text:
curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_20.x | sudo -E bash -
sudo apt-get install -y nodejs

docs.title: 2. Clone and Install
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/SomeshwarSutar/my-nextjs-app.git
cd my-nextjs-app
npm install

docs.title: 3. Configure Environment
docs.text:
cp .env.example .env
nano .env   # Fill in DATABASE_URL, NEXTAUTH_SECRET, NEXTAUTH_URL

docs.title: 4. Run Migrations
docs.text:
npx prisma migrate deploy
npm run seed   # Only for first-time setup

docs.title: 5. Build the Application
docs.text: npm run build

docs.title: 6. Start the Production Server
docs.text:
npm run start

The app will listen on port 3000 by default.

docs.title: 7. Set Up a Reverse Proxy (Nginx)
docs.text:
server {
    listen 80;
    server_name myblog.example.com;

    location / {
        proxy_pass http://localhost:3000;
        proxy_http_version 1.1;
        proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade;
        proxy_set_header Connection 'upgrade';
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
        proxy_cache_bypass $http_upgrade;
    }
}

Enable HTTPS with Certbot:

sudo certbot --nginx -d myblog.example.com

docs.title: 8. Run with PM2 (Process Manager)
docs.text:
npm install -g pm2
pm2 start npm --name "my-blog-app" -- start
pm2 save
pm2 startup

docs.title: Option 3: Docker
docs.text:
A Dockerfile is not yet included in this repository. A basic example:

FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci
COPY . .
RUN npx prisma generate
RUN npm run build

FROM node:20-alpine AS runner
WORKDIR /app
ENV NODE_ENV=production
COPY --from=builder /app/.next ./.next
COPY --from=builder /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY --from=builder /app/package.json ./package.json
COPY --from=builder /app/prisma ./prisma
EXPOSE 3000
CMD ["npm", "run", "start"]

docs.title: Post-Deployment Checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] NEXTAUTH_SECRET is set to a strong, unique value
  • [ ] NEXTAUTH_URL matches the production domain exactly
  • [ ] Database migrations have been applied (npx prisma migrate deploy)
  • [ ] Default admin password has been changed
  • [ ] HTTPS is enabled
  • [ ] Database is not publicly accessible (use internal networking or firewall rules)

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: The application exposes a REST API under /api. All endpoints return JSON.

docs.title: GET /api/posts
docs.text: Returns a paginated list of published posts, with optional full-text search.

docs.title: Query Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Default Description page integer 1 Page number (1-based) limit integer 10 Number of posts per page q string "" Search query (matches title, excerpt, and content)

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text: GET /api/posts?page=1&limit=5&q=nextjs

docs.title: Example Response
docs.text:
{
  "items": [
    {
      "id": 3,
      "title": "Getting Started with Next.js",
      "slug": "getting-started-with-nextjs",
      "excerpt": "A beginner's guide to building with Next.js.",
      "publishedAt": "2026-05-20T10:00:00.000Z",
      "viewCount": 142,
      "isFeatured": false
    }
  ],
  "total": 1,
  "page": 1,
  "limit": 5,
  "totalPages": 1
}

docs.title: Response Fields
docs.text: Field Type Description items array Array of post objects items[].id integer Unique post identifier items[].title string Post title items[].slug string URL-friendly post identifier items[].excerpt string \| null Short description of the post items[].publishedAt string \| null ISO 8601 publish timestamp items[].viewCount integer Total view count items[].isFeatured boolean Whether the post is featured total integer Total number of matching posts page integer Current page limit integer Posts per page totalPages integer Total number of pages

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text: Authentication is handled by NextAuth.js at /api/auth/[...nextauth].

docs.title: POST /api/auth/signin
docs.text: Sign in with credentials (email and password).

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text:
{
  "email": "admin@someshwarsutar.com",
  "password": "Admin@123"
}

docs.title: Successful Response
docs.text: Returns a session cookie and redirects (or returns url) on success.

docs.title: Error Response
docs.text: Returns { "error": "CredentialsSignin" } if the credentials are invalid.

docs.title: Session Object
docs.text:
After a successful login, the session includes the user's role:

{
  "user": {
    "name": "Super Admin",
    "email": "admin@someshwarsutar.com",
    "role": "ADMIN"
  },
  "expires": "2026-07-01T00:00:00.000Z"
}

docs.title: POST /api/auth/signout
docs.text: Signs the current user out and clears the session.

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
All endpoints return standard HTTP status codes:

Status Meaning 200 OK Request succeeded 400 Bad Request Invalid query parameters 401 Unauthorized Authentication required 403 Forbidden Insufficient permissions 404 Not Found Resource not found 500 Internal Server Error Unexpected server error

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: All runtime configuration is managed through environment variables in a .env file at the project root.

docs.title: Database
docs.text: Variable Description Example DATABASE_URL Prisma-compatible connection string for MariaDB/MySQL mysql://user:pass@localhost:3306/blog_db

docs.title: Authentication (NextAuth.js)
docs.text: Variable Description Example NEXTAUTH_SECRET Secret key used to sign JWTs and encrypt cookies. Required in production. A 32+ character random string NEXTAUTH_URL The canonical URL of your application http://localhost:3000

docs.title: Example .env File
docs.text:
# Database
DATABASE_URL="mysql://blog_user:strong_password@localhost:3306/blog_db"

# NextAuth
NEXTAUTH_SECRET="your-super-secret-key-here"
NEXTAUTH_URL="http://localhost:3000"

docs.title: Generating a Secure Secret
docs.text:
Use the following command to generate a strong NEXTAUTH_SECRET:

openssl rand -base64 32

docs.title: Next.js Configuration
docs.text:
The next.config.ts file at the project root is used for Next.js-specific settings such as image domains, redirects, and headers.

// next.config.ts
import type { NextConfig } from "next";

const nextConfig: NextConfig = {
  /* Add your config options here */
};

export default nextConfig;

docs.title: Prisma Configuration
docs.text:
The prisma/schema.prisma file defines the database provider and data models. The prisma.config.ts file at the root can hold additional Prisma CLI configuration.

After any schema change, regenerate the Prisma client:

npx prisma generate

Apply changes to the database:

npx prisma migrate dev --name <migration-name>

docs.title: Tailwind CSS
docs.text: Tailwind is configured via postcss.config.mjs. DaisyUI is included as a Tailwind plugin and can be themed in src/app/globals.css.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: This guide walks you through cloning the repository, installing dependencies, and running the application locally.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: Tool Minimum Version Node.js 20.x npm 9.x MariaDB or MySQL 10.6+ / 8.0+

docs.title: 1. Clone the Repository
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/SomeshwarSutar/my-nextjs-app.git
cd my-nextjs-app

docs.title: 2. Install Dependencies
docs.text: npm install

docs.title: 3. Configure Environment Variables
docs.text:
Copy the example environment file and fill in your values:

cp .env.example .env

Edit .env with your database credentials and secrets. See Configuration for all available variables.

docs.title: 4. Set Up the Database
docs.text:
Run Prisma migrations to create the database schema:

npx prisma migrate deploy

Seed the database with an initial admin user:

npm run seed

This creates the following admin account:

Field Value Email admin@someshwarsutar.com Password Admin@123 Role ADMIN

Important: Change the admin password immediately after your first login in a production environment.

docs.title: 5. Run the Development Server
docs.text:
npm run dev

The application will be available at http://localhost:3000.

docs.title: 6. Available Scripts
docs.text: Script Description npm run dev Start the development server with hot-reload npm run build Compile the application for production npm run start Start the production server npm run lint Run ESLint across the codebase npm run seed Seed the database with initial data

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Configure environment variables
  • Explore the API
  • Learn about the Admin Panel

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Common issues and their solutions.

docs.title: Error: Can't reach database server
docs.text:
Cause: The database is not running or the connection string is incorrect.

Fix: 1. Ensure MariaDB/MySQL is running: sudo systemctl status mariadb 2. Verify your DATABASE_URL in .env matches your database host, port, credentials, and database name. 3. Confirm the database exists: SHOW DATABASES; in your MariaDB client.

docs.title: PrismaClientInitializationError: Prisma Client is not initialized
docs.text:
Cause: The Prisma client has not been generated after a schema change or fresh install.

Fix:

npx prisma generate

docs.title: Redirect loop on /login
docs.text:
Cause: NEXTAUTH_URL does not match the actual URL you're accessing.

Fix: Set NEXTAUTH_URL in .env to exactly match the URL in your browser, including the port:

NEXTAUTH_URL="http://localhost:3000"

docs.title: [next-auth][error][JWT_SESSION_ERROR]
docs.text:
Cause: NEXTAUTH_SECRET is missing or has changed since existing sessions were created.

Fix: 1. Ensure NEXTAUTH_SECRET is set in .env. 2. If you changed the secret, existing sessions are invalidated — users must log in again.

docs.title: Login fails with "Invalid credentials"
docs.text:
Cause: The email or password does not match any user in the database.

Fix: - If this is a fresh install, run npm run seed to create the default admin account. - Reset the password by running a custom Prisma script or using npx prisma studio.

docs.title: Module not found: Can't resolve '@/lib/...'
docs.text:
Cause: TypeScript path aliases are not configured.

Fix: Verify tsconfig.json contains the path alias:

{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "paths": {
      "@/*": ["./src/*"]
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Type error: Property 'role' does not exist on type 'User'
docs.text:
Cause: NextAuth type augmentation is missing.

Fix: Create or update src/types/next-auth.d.ts:

import "next-auth";

declare module "next-auth" {
  interface User {
    role: string;
  }
  interface Session {
    user: User & {
      role: string;
    };
  }
}

declare module "next-auth/jwt" {
  interface JWT {
    role: string;
  }
}

docs.title: Migration failed to apply cleanly
docs.text:
Fix: Reset the development database and re-apply migrations (data will be lost):

npx prisma migrate reset

docs.title: Still Stuck?
docs.text:
• Check the Next.js docs
  • Check the Prisma docs
  • Check the NextAuth.js docs
  • Open an issue on GitHub
